title: Similarity (Length, Area, Volume Ratios) — PYP Mastery

questions.question: A car is an enlargement of a model, the scale factor being^10. If the windscreen of the model has an area of $100\text{ cm}^2$, find the area of the windscreen on the actual car in $\text{m}^2$.
questions.explanation:
Scale factor $k = 10$, so area ratio $k^2 = 100$. Area of car windscreen $= 100 \times 100 = 10000\text{ cm}^2$. Since $1\text{ m}^2 = 10000\text{ cm}^2$, the area is $1\text{ m}^2$.

**Common mistake:** The student may correctly find^10000 but fail to convert $\text{cm}^2$ to $\text{m}^2$ correctly (dividing by^100 instead of $100^2$).

questions.question: The surface areas of two similar jugs are $50\text{ cm}^2$ and $450\text{ cm}^2$ respectively. If the height of the larger jug is $10\text{ cm}$, find the height of the smaller jug.
questions.explanation:
Area ratio $k^2 = 450/50 = 9$. Linear scale factor $k = 3$. Height of smaller jug $= 10 / 3 \approx 3.33\text{ cm}$.

**Common mistake:** Dividing the height by the area ratio ($9$) instead of the linear scale factor ($3$).

questions.question: A conical tank full of acid is similar to a conical beaker. The beaker can be filled from the tank exactly^1728 times. If the base radius of the beaker is $8\text{ cm}$, find the radius of the tank.
questions.explanation:
Volume ratio $k^3 = 1728$. Linear scale factor $k = \sqrt{1728} = 12$. Tank radius $= 8 \times 12 = 96\text{ cm}$.

**Common mistake:** Dividing the radius by 12 or attempting to use the square root instead of the cube root.

questions.question: In triangle $PQR$ and $S2PR$ and $T2PQ$. $ST2RQ$. So $PT = 5\text{ cm}$ and $TQ = 2\text{ cm}$, and the length of $ST$. $4\text{ cm}$, calculate the length of $RQ$.
questions.explanation:
$PQ = 5 + 2 = 7\text{ cm}$. Linear scale factor $k = PQ/PT = 7/5 = 1.4$. Therefore, $RQ = 4 \times 1.4 = 5.6\text{ cm}$.

**Common mistake:** Using the segment $TQ$. $2\text{ cm}$) in the ratio calculation instead of the full side length $PQ$.

questions.question: The radii of two spheres are in the ratio $2 : 5$. If the volume of the smaller sphere is $16\text{ cm}^3$, calculate the volume of the larger sphere.
questions.explanation:
Linear ratio is $2 : 5$. Volume ratio is $2^3 : 5^3 = 8 : 125$. Larger volume $= 16 \times (125 / 8) = 2 \times 125 = 250\text{ cm}^3$.

**Common mistake:** Squaring the radius ratio ($4 : 25$) and applying it to volume, resulting in $100\text{ cm}^3$.
